title: salix pseudocordata var. aequalis
intro: Salix pseudocordata var. aequalis, also known as riverbank willow, is a variety of the willow plant that is native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and fight off infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides a range of health benefits including reducing fever, relieving arthritis symptoms, and promoting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of riverbank willow can be made by steeping the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by infusing the plant in a carrier oil to create a topical salve.
